AD_FIELD.NAME: Client
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_FIELD.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Name
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A non-unique identifier for a record/document often used as a search tool.
AD_FIELD.HELP: A more descriptive identifier (that does need to be unique) of a record/document that is used as a default search option along with the search key (that is unique and mostly shorter). It is up to 60 characters in length.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Active
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_FIELD.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Table
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A dictionary table used for this tab that points to the database table.
AD_FIELD.HELP: The Table indicates the table in which a field or fields reside.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Description
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A space to write additional related information.
AD_FIELD.HELP: A description is limited to 255 characters.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Subject
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The Subject of the mail
AD_FIELD.HELP: References will be replaced by values: * @cus_ref@: The document reference of the customer * @our_ref@: The reference of the document * @cus_nam@: The name of the customer * @sal_nam@: The name of the sales rep. * @bp_nam@: The Business Partner name * @doc_date@: The document date * @doc_desc@: The document description * @doc_nextduedate@: The next due date (if document has associated any payment plan) * @doc_lastduedate@: The last due date (if document has associated any payment plan)

AD_FIELD.NAME: Commercial Name
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Commercial Name of the Business Partner
AD_FIELD.HELP: Commercial Name of the Business Partner

AD_FIELD.NAME: Table
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A dictionary table used for this tab that points to the database table.
AD_FIELD.HELP: The Table indicates the table in which a field or fields reside.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Client
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_FIELD.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Active
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_FIELD.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Active
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_FIELD.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Active
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_FIELD.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Timeout (seconds)
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The timeout is the frequency (in seconds) which the ping for a cluster service is done.
AD_FIELD.HELP: The timeout is the frequency (in seconds) which the ping for a cluster service is done, i.e., it represents the amount of time that should pass for the cluster node in charge of a cluster service to reaffirm itself as the leader. It is also used as a reference for the rest of the nodes in the cluster to determine if the leader is no longer alive and therefore if a new node should be promoted as the leader of the cluster service. Please note that it will be required to restart all the nodes in the cluster if this value is changed in order to make the new value visible for the entire cluster.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Organization
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Organizational entity within client
AD_FIELD.HELP: An organization is a unit of your client or legal entity - examples are store, department. You can share data between organizations.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Active
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_FIELD.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Active
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A flag indicating whether this record is available for use or de-activated.
AD_FIELD.HELP: There are two methods of making records unavailable in the system: One is to delete the record, the other is to de-activate the record. A de-activated record is not available for selection, but available for reporting. There are two reasons for de-activating and not deleting records: (1) The system requires the record for auditing purposes. (2) The record is referenced by other records. E.g., you cannot delete a Business Partner, if there are existing invoices for it. By de-activating the Business Partner you prevent it from being used in future transactions.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Subject
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: The Subject of the mail
AD_FIELD.HELP: References will be replaced by values: * @cus_ref@: The document reference of the customer * @our_ref@: The reference of the document * @cus_nam@: The name of the customer * @sal_nam@: The name of the sales rep. * @bp_nam@: The Business Partner name * @doc_date@: The document date * @doc_desc@: The document description * @doc_nextduedate@: The next due date (if document has associated any payment plan) * @doc_lastduedate@: The last due date (if document has associated any payment plan)

AD_FIELD.NAME: Client
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_FIELD.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Username
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: User Name
AD_FIELD.HELP: Name that identifies the user in the System

AD_FIELD.NAME: Default
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A value that is shown whenever a record is created.
AD_FIELD.HELP: The Default Checkbox indicates if this record will be used as a default value.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Tab
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: An indication that a tab is displayed within a window.
AD_FIELD.HELP: The Tab indicates a tab that displays within a window.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Client
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_FIELD.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Search Key
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A fast method for finding a particular record.
AD_FIELD.HELP:
A search key allows for a fast method of finding a particular record. If you leave the search key empty, the system automatically creates a numeric number originating from a document sequence defined in the "Document Sequence" window. The sequence naming convention follows a rule "DocumentNo_TableName", where TableName is the actual name of the database table (e.g. C_Order).
Usually, company's internal identifiers for various records (products, customers, etc) are stored here.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Table
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A dictionary table used for this tab that points to the database table.
AD_FIELD.HELP: The Table indicates the table in which a field or fields reside.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Description
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A space to write additional related information.
AD_FIELD.HELP: A description is limited to 255 characters.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Alert Status
AD_FIELD.HELP: Status of the alert: New: The alert has been created and has not been reviewed yet. Read: The alert has been reviewed and user is waiting to be solved. Solved: The alert has been solved and is not an issue any more. Exception: The alert has been acknowledged but not action is going to be made to solve it.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Client
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_FIELD.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Client
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Client for this installation.
AD_FIELD.HELP: A Client is a company or a legal entity. You cannot share data between Clients.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Smtp Connection Security
AD_FIELD.HELP: Connection security needed for the defined smtp server.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Organization
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: Organizational entity within client
AD_FIELD.HELP: An organization is a unit of your client or legal entity - examples are store, department. You can share data between organizations.

AD_FIELD.NAME: Password
AD_FIELD.DESCRIPTION: A secret code used to allow access to a specified window or tab.
AD_FIELD.HELP: The Password indicates the Password for this User Id. Passwords are required to identify authorized users.
